title: Smt Ashabi W/O Khalandar Khan vs Krishnappa S/O Govindappa Udupi on 5 September, 2013
body: Smt Ashabi W/O Khalandar Khan vs Krishnappa S/O Govindappa Udupi on 5 September, 2013 Author: S.N.Satyanarayana Bench: S.N.Satyanarayana 1 IN THE HIGH COURT OF KARNATAKA DHARWAD BENCH DATED THIS THE 5TH DAY OF SEPTEMBER, 2013 BEFORE THE HON'BLE MR.JUSTICE S.N.SATYANARAYANA R.S.A. SHAKUNTAL W/O NARAYAN UDUPI AGE: MAJOR, OCC: HOUSEHOLD POLICE QUARTERS, KOPPA-577126 1(F) PUSHAPA DEVENDRA UDUPI, C/O VASANT KRISHANAPPA UDUPI, A C COMPOUND CANTEEN, SAVANUR, DIST: HAVERI-581 118. 1(G) SUDHAKAR KRISHANAPPA UDUPI C/O VASANT KRISHANAPPA UDUPI A C COMPOUND CANTEEN, SAVANUR, DIST: HAVERI-581118. ... RESPONDENTS [R.1(A)-R1(G) brought on record vide C.O.Dated.19.4.12 as LR of R.1] (By Sri HEMANTH CHANDANAGOUDAR, ADV. FOR R.1(A)-R.1(G)) THIS RSA IS FILED UNDER SECTION 100 CPC , PRAYING TO SET-ASIDE JUDGEMENT AND DECREE DATED:14.10.2009, PASSED IN R.A.NO.2/2004, ON THE FILE OF THE ADDL.CIVIL JUDGE (SR.DN) HAVERI, AND ORDER OF CIVIL JUDGE (JR.DN) SAVANUR DATED:17.11.2003, PASSED IN CIVIL MISC.NO.1/99, AND ETC. THIS RSA COMING ON FOR ADMISSION, THIS DAY, THE COURT DELIVERED THE FOLLOWING: JUDGMENT This 2nd appeal is by the applicants in Civl.Misc.1/1999 dated 17.11.2003 on the file of the Civil Judge (Jr.Dn), 5 Savanur, seeking mesne profits for having lost possession of suit schedule property in O. S.112 /1979 for the period from 19.10.1982 till 12.6.1998. 2. The brief facts leading to this second appeal are as under: The appellants' predecessor, namely Khalandar Khan was said to be tenant in respect of suit schedule property in O. S.112 / 79 . The suit for possession was filed by respondent namely, Krishnappa against him. The said suit was decreed on 27.8.82, against which the defendant Khalandar Khan filed appeal in R.A.51/1982 and the said appeal was admitted and judgment and decree in O. S.112 / 79 was stayed by order dated 19.10.1982. In the meanwhile, plaintiff in the original suit levied execution in E.P.47/82 and secured possession of suit schedule property on 20.10.1982. 3. Subsequently it is seen that R.A.51/1982 filed by defendant in the original suit was allowed, against which the plaintiff preferred the Second Appeal in RSA.7/85 which was 6 dismissed by judgment and decree dated 4.1.1985. Thereafter, the defendants in O. S.112 / 79 filed miscellaneous proceedings in Misc.Pet.8/1984 u/s 144 of CPC seeking restoration of possession, which came to be allowed and the same was challenged by plaintiff in RA.14/85 which was allowed and order passed in RA.8/84 is set aside. The same was challenged by appellants herein in RSA.53/1987 which came to be allowed on 15.7.1997 in confirming the order of trial Court passed in Misc.P.8/1984 and the order passed in RA.14/85 preferred by respondents was reversed. Consequently possession was restored to defendant in O. S.112 /1979 on 12.6.1985. 4. Subsequent to that, Misc.No.1/1999 was filed seeking mesne profits by defendant in original suit for having lost his possession for the period from 20.10.1982 to 12.6.1998. The said miscellaneous proceedings came to be dismissed on the ground that it was barred by limitation, for the reason that the limitation to seek said relief commence from the date of disposal of RSA.7/85 on 4.1.1985, under 7 which the possession is secured by defendant. Therefore he should have filed the application seeking mesne profits immediately after securing possession, instead of filing it in the year 1999, that is after 12 years, and therefore the right to seek mesne profits was not subsisting as on the date of filing of application in Misc.No.1/1999. The said finding of the trial court is accepted by the lower appellate court while deciding RA.2/2004 on the file of Addl. Civil Judge (Sr.Dn) & JMFC, Haveri. 5. As against the concurrent findings of both the courts below this second appeal is filed. In this appeal, the point which arise for consideration is in narrow compass as to: "Whether the application filed in Misc.No.1/1999 is well within the period of limitation?" Admittedly, Misc.No.1/99 is filed on 18.1.1999. The right to file this application arose to defendant in O. S.112 / 79 in 1985. Therefore, by virtue of that within three years application should have been filed by him seeking mesne 8 profits for the period during which he was illegally dispossessed. Further it is seen that the said application is filed after nearly 12 years that is beyond the period of limitation. In that view of the matter, dismissal of his application filed in Misc.No.1/99 appears to be correct. It is also seen that the finding of lower appellate court in RA.2/2004 in not entertaining the said appeal and dismissing the same in confirming the order dated 17.11.2003 passed in Civ.Misc.No.1/1999 appears to be just and proper. 6. In that view of the matter, this court find the concurrent finding of both courts below in dismissing the application of appellant herein for the relief of mesne profits appears to be just and proper. Therefore, no grounds are made out to admit this second appeal, in as much as, no substantial question of law arise for consideration. Accordingly, the appeal is dismissed. Sd/- JUDGE .sub/
